task: Test the notification system. When person a in the house hold adds something assigned to person b then person b should get a notification. Also when person b completes, person a should get completed alert. Similarly test other possible alerts.

data.plan: Implement and test a comprehensive notification system for household member interactions. This involves: (1) Creating/updating API routes for push notifications when items are assigned or completed, (2) Adding server-side logic to send push notifications to the relevant household member when a chore/reminder/grocery/meal is assigned to them, (3) Adding notifications back to the assigner when an assignee completes a task, (4) Writing integration and unit tests covering assignment notifications, completion notifications, and other alert scenarios (e.g., due date reminders, grocery added, event created). The notification-bell, push-send, and push-subscribe routes will be updated or verified to support these flows. Test files will be added/updated under src/__tests__/notifications/ to cover all scenarios using MSW mocks and the existing Supabase mock infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: When person A assigns a chore to person B, person B receives a push notification with the chore title and assigner name
When person B completes a chore assigned by person A, person A receives a push notification confirming completion
When person A assigns a reminder to person B, person B receives a push notification
When person B completes a reminder, person A receives a completion push notification
When a grocery item is added and assigned to a household member, that member receives a push notification
When a grocery item is marked as purchased, the creator/assigner receives a notification
When a meal is planned and assigned to a person, that person receives a notification
When an event is created and assigned to household members, those members receive notifications
Notifications are only sent to users who have active push subscriptions stored in the database
If a user has no push subscription, the notification silently skips without throwing errors
The notification bell endpoint returns unread notification count and list for the authenticated user
Push subscriptions are correctly stored and retrieved per user from Supabase
All notification test scenarios pass with mocked Supabase and web-push dependencies
Notifications include relevant metadata: title, body, icon, and a deep-link URL to the relevant page
Self-assignments (person A assigns to themselves) do not send a duplicate notification
The push/send API route is protected and only callable server-side or by authenticated users
